name: TB-500
summary: TB-500 is a synthetic fragment of thymosin beta-4 marketed for muscle recovery and connective-tissue repair; the underlying Tβ4 biology has real scientific grounding, but the synthetic fragment sold as TB-500 has thin direct human evidence and an unresolved FDA compounding status pending July 2026 PCAC review.
description: TB-500 is a synthetic fragment of thymosin beta-4 (Tβ4), a naturally occurring peptide found in nearly all human and animal cells. It's marketed primarily around muscle recovery, flexibility, and connective-tissue repair, and is often stacked with BPC-157 in gray-market use despite acting through a different mechanism (actin regulation rather than angiogenesis/NO signaling). It is not FDA-approved for human use; product sold online is almost universally labeled research use only.
foundIn: Endogenous thymosin beta-4 (nearly all human and animal cells)
bioavailability: Reported via subcutaneous injection in RUO products; human pharmacokinetic data for the synthetic fragment is not established.
safetyNotes: No controlled human safety data exists. Theoretical caution around growth-promoting peptide biology and cancer history warrants physician oversight rather than self-directed use.
